jokes.text: How come you can't trust me? - Because I grew up with you.
jokes.setup: Will protests about not being trusted
jokes.punchline: Jackie's blunt response based on their shared history
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical punchline with strong character integration - Jackie knows Will too well to trust him

jokes.text: Hey, look, you are talking to one tired freshman here, Miss Boss Lady.
jokes.setup: Will has been cleaning everything
jokes.punchline: His playfully dramatic complaint about being overworked
jokes.explanation: Standard Will character beat - his street attitude clashing with domestic responsibilities

jokes.text: Hey, you know, Agnes. I don't know why your daughter doesn't call you.
jokes.setup: Will is talking to random customers
jokes.punchline: Overly familiar commentary to a stranger about their family problems
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's inappropriate overfamiliarity with customers

jokes.text: You went through it with Carlton. Yes, and look at how well he turned out.
jokes.setup: Philip reassures Vivian about separation anxiety
jokes.punchline: Vivian's deadpan response about Carlton's outcome
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery undermining Philip's reassurance about their parenting success

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, I think you should be more concerned with a cholesterol-free good time.
jokes.setup: Philip talks about romantic plans
jokes.punchline: Will's dig at Philip's weight/health
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Big poopy. - What did you say? - Oh, I said: 'Beg, puppy'... to my pretend dog, Ernie.
jokes.setup: Will says something that sounds inappropriate
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous cover story about a pretend dog
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with Will's increasingly elaborate lie about the pretend dog

jokes.text: Fetch, Ernie. He's gone now.
jokes.setup: Will continues the pretend dog charade
jokes.punchline: Commitment to the bit with the dog 'leaving'
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-through on the pretend dog bit - shows Will's commitment to absurd lies

jokes.text: Who's Bobby?
jokes.setup: Vivian spells out B-A-B-Y to hide meaning
jokes.punchline: Will misunderstands the spelling as a name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection on the spelling gag

jokes.text: We'll draw straws for the little bugger. Short one loses.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offers to care for the baby
jokes.punchline: His callous suggestion to gamble over childcare duties
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey character moment - his dry British cynicism about childcare

jokes.text: I've got a date with Mr. Stinky!
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to get out of babysitting duty
jokes.punchline: His overly enthusiastic reference to changing diapers
jokes.explanation: Carlton's characteristic awkward enthusiasm about an unpleasant task

jokes.text: Well, I'm hoping you like it dark, hot, sweet... and with a spoon in it.
jokes.setup: Will describes what sounds like a romantic preference
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he's talking about coffee
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup - audience thinks he's being suggestive until the spoon reveal

jokes.text: He reminds me of Trevor.
jokes.setup: Hilary describes baby Nicky watching her undress and drooling
jokes.punchline: The comparison to her deceased fiancé Trevor
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny callback to Trevor's death while also being characteristically inappropriate for Hilary

jokes.text: Wipe my booty.
jokes.setup: Will lists baby care complaints
jokes.punchline: The final, most graphic item on his list
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation to crude baby care reality - functional but not sophisticated

jokes.text: Well, like padre, like nino, you know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Customer compliments the 'adorable' baby
jokes.punchline: Will's bilingual twist on 'like father, like son'
jokes.explanation: Clever linguistic play showing Will's street-smart adaptation of common phrases

jokes.text: All right, break it up. Move on. Nothing to see here. Show's over. Beat it!
jokes.setup: Customer commenting on Will's adorable 'son'
jokes.punchline: Will acting like crowd control to disperse attention
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Will's panic at unwanted attention

jokes.text: Since Tirisha died.
jokes.setup: Will improvises being a single father
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of a tragic backstory
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation - Will casually inventing a dead girlfriend for sympathy

jokes.text: Hey! Isn't that who played Greg Brady?! - Where? Hey, Greg, wait up!
jokes.setup: Hilary is about to expose Will's lie
jokes.punchline: Perfect distraction with Brady Bunch reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect sitcom timing with the celebrity distraction saving Will from exposure

jokes.text: And she can sleep over any time she wants to.
jokes.setup: Will tells the baby they made a new friend
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate addition about sleepovers
jokes.explanation: Will's characteristic horniness breaking through his fake father persona

jokes.text: 'The Wheels on the Bus Go Round and Round.'
jokes.setup: Philip mentions requesting Vivian's favorite song
jokes.punchline: The reveal that it's a children's song
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how baby-obsessed Vivian has become

jokes.text: Excuse me, but when did I get old enough to hear this?
jokes.setup: Philip mentions romantic history at Mario's
jokes.punchline: Will breaking the fourth wall about inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment with Will acknowledging the inappropriateness for his character age

jokes.text: I don't know.
jokes.setup: Parents ask where the baby is
jokes.punchline: Hilary's casual admission of not knowing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - complete obliviousness delivered with casual confidence

jokes.text: Oh, God, it's not like I left him at Sizzler... Dad.
jokes.setup: Hilary tries to defend losing the baby
jokes.punchline: Her specific reference to Sizzler as if that would be worse
jokes.explanation: Great Hilary logic - the specific restaurant choice makes her defense even more absurd

jokes.text: No, Uncle Phil, you got at least five years before you need them.
jokes.setup: Philip jokes about wearing diapers for attention
jokes.punchline: Will's dig about Philip's age/health trajectory
jokes.explanation: Another fat/age joke about Philip - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Thanks for the warning, Captain Weenie.
jokes.setup: Carlton threatens to call their parents
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive nickname for Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good Will/Carlton dynamic with the perfect dismissive nickname

jokes.text: You just an embarrassment, man.
jokes.setup: Will calls the situation an embarrassment of riches
jokes.punchline: His brutal dismissal of his coworker
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay with Will turning the phrase against his coworker

jokes.text: And I'd like to say, 'Hey, I'm here for you'... but, heck, I'd be lying.
jokes.setup: Jackie sees Will is in trouble
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission of not wanting to help
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan honesty from Jackie - subverts expectation of supportive friendship

jokes.text: I'm saying, you know I can't be trusted. You wouldn't have left me here, none of this would ever happen.
jokes.setup: Jackie blames Will for the mess
jokes.punchline: Will's twisted logic making it Jackie's fault for trusting him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the opening trust issue with Will's characteristic blame-shifting logic

jokes.text: What?!
jokes.setup: Candace arrives at a bad moment
jokes.punchline: Will's explosive, inappropriate greeting
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Will's frazzled state affecting his smooth persona

jokes.text: Deficiency, you know, from my night job at the target range.
jokes.setup: Will covers for his yelling with hearing problems
jokes.punchline: His elaborate cover story about a target range job
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's quick thinking under pressure with increasingly elaborate lies

jokes.text: Well, he just a baby. How important could his plans be?
jokes.setup: Candace mentions the baby has plans
jokes.punchline: Will's logical but dismissive response about baby priorities
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about baby scheduling priorities

jokes.text: All I need is me a baby.
jokes.setup: Will celebrates getting a woman
jokes.punchline: His realization he needs to procure a baby for his lie
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the escalating deception with Will's matter-of-fact problem-solving

jokes.text: I've seen you both naked anyway.
jokes.setup: Philip and Vivian are being romantic
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's inappropriate observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - inappropriate honesty delivered with British matter-of-factness

jokes.text: Yes, well, you just lay back and relax and let Daddy take care of you.
jokes.setup: Philip tries to be romantic
jokes.punchline: His awkward daddy reference given the baby context
jokes.explanation: Mildly cringey given the baby-obsessed context making 'daddy' awkward

jokes.text: A zucchini. A zucchini, Will.
jokes.setup: Carlton found a substitute baby
jokes.punchline: His dramatic repetition of the vegetable choice
jokes.explanation: Absurdist perfection - Carlton's moral outrage over a zucchini baby substitute is hilariously specific

jokes.text: There's a world of change out there, mister... and I'm going to find it.
jokes.setup: Carlton needs change for the phone
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration about finding change in the world
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - turning a mundane need for change into an epic quest

jokes.text: Yeah, we came here straight from church.
jokes.setup: Will arrives for his fake father TV appearance
jokes.punchline: His lie about coming from church to add respectability
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Will's automatic embellishment even when unnecessary

jokes.text: Sorry, we're closed. Be open at 10.
jokes.setup: Will is asked about being excited on TV
jokes.punchline: His panicked reversion to coffee shop employee mode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant panic response - Will's brain short-circuits and defaults to work mode

jokes.text: Olive Oyl is looking awfully good right now.
jokes.setup: Philip is frustrated watching cartoons instead of being romantic
jokes.punchline: His admission that even cartoon Olive Oyl is appealing
jokes.explanation: Good frustrated husband humor with the specific cartoon reference

jokes.text: That's my zucchini.
jokes.setup: Vivian sees the zucchini baby substitute on TV
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback escalation - Vivian's casual recognition of her vegetable on television

jokes.text: General Powell said to me: 'Yo, homey, you need to get down with a little R and R... because you're looking a little singed.'
jokes.setup: Will invents a military backstory
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous quote from General Powell using street slang
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - General Powell speaking like Will is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: It was in a little-known province called... Rae Dawn Chong.
jokes.setup: Will describes meeting Tirisha in China
jokes.punchline: His naming a province after an actress
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist comedy - the specific actress choice and Will's confidence in the delivery make this exceptional

jokes.text: Well, sayonara.
jokes.setup: Will is lost in his fake China story
jokes.punchline: His use of Japanese instead of Chinese farewell
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's geographical confusion - wrong Asian language caps the absurdity

jokes.text: Hawaii too?
jokes.setup: Will gives away all the prizes to the real single father
jokes.punchline: His momentary hesitation about giving up Hawaii
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's brief temptation before doing the right thing

jokes.text: See... this ain't my kid either. Aloha.
jokes.setup: Todd seems like the deserving real single father
jokes.punchline: His revelation that he's also lying, then leaving for Hawaii
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double-twist ending - subverts the redemptive moment with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: A zucchi... A zucchini.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to tell the story again
jokes.punchline: His continued obsession with the zucchini detail
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Carlton still traumatized by the zucchini substitution

jokes.text: Good luck, Captain Weenie.
jokes.setup: Carlton continues his complaint
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive callback to his earlier nickname
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the Captain Weenie nickname, bookending their conflict
